name: Least Privilege Violation
description: The product retains elevated privileges longer than necessary after performing a privileged operation. The elevated privilege level required for operations like chroot() should be dropped immediately after the operation completes, but the product continues running with unnecessary elevated access.
detection_pattern: Look for AUTH nodes (elevated privilege acquisition, root/admin context) that enable a CONTROL or TRANSFORM node (privileged operation like chroot, bind to port, file open) but continue to flow to subsequent TRANSFORM, EXTERNAL, or CONTROL nodes (normal operations, user-facing code) without a CONTROL mediator (immediate privilege drop, setuid/setgid, capability restriction) that reduces privileges right after the privileged operation.
